blurb:
Chocolate Orange is a young female cat with a name that probably describes her coloring perfectly, though her listing doesn't give us much to work with personality-wise. She's at that great age where she's past the kitten craziness but still has plenty of energy and curiosity left.

Young cats like this are usually the sweet spot for adoption. They're old enough to have developed their personality but young enough to adapt to new homes easily. They're typically playful without being overwhelming and affectionate without being clingy. She's probably somewhere between playful and chill, which makes for a great companion.

Since her bio is pretty bare bones, you'll definitely want to meet her in person to see what she's all about. Some cats are social butterflies, others are more reserved, and a few minutes with her will tell you which camp she falls into.

Chocolate Orange is at DeKalb County Animal Services in Chamblee. Give them a call to check on her availability and find out what their adoption process looks like.
scoring_notes:
• Ideal age range appeals to wide variety of potential adopters
• County shelter location creates urgency for placement
• Lacks compelling narrative to distinguish from other available cats

blurb: Harvey came to the rescue with his two buddies when their owner passed away. One of them had originally been adopted from the same rescue as a puppy, so when it came time to help, they took in all three dogs because nobody gets left behind. Harvey is the chill one of the group. He's got short little legs on a low rider body that makes him extra adorable, and despite having some gray on his face, he's only about three years old. He gets along great with all dogs and previously lived with cats, so he's pretty adaptable. The rescue has no idea what breed he is, but honestly, that just adds to his charm. He's the kind of dog who's going to be happy wherever he lands as long as there's a couch to claim and maybe a friend or two around. If you're looking for a laid-back companion who's already proven he can roll with whatever life throws at him, Harvey might be your guy. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application at www.rmgdogs.org to get the process started.
scoring_notes:
• Heartwarming loyalty story of rescue taking in all three dogs together
• Easy-going personality and adaptability make him highly adoptable
• Unique low-rider appearance and mystery breed add reader appeal

blurb:
Beignet was adopted from Good Mews as a kitten, and somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter. Nobody knows what happened in between, but the shelter called Good Mews and they brought her back immediately. She's been in foster care getting her confidence back, and it worked. She went from shy to sweet pretty quickly.

She loves being petted and is what they call a social eater. She wants company while she eats, which is actually kind of endearing. When she wants attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, so she needs someone who knows cats well enough to read her signals and back off when she's had enough.

This is her second chance at finding the right home. The first one didn't work out for whatever reason, but that doesn't mean the next one won't be great. Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet her. Call or email to get started.
scoring_notes:
• Compelling mystery story of abandonment and rescue creates strong emotional connection
• Second chance narrative with redemptive arc appeals to readers' empathy
• Needs experienced cat owner which may limit adoption pool

blurb: When Morrie showed up in late fall, he was in rough shape. Extremely thin with a severe skin infection from what was likely years of neglect. It took months of care in his foster home to get him healthy and ready for adoption. Now he's about 18 pounds and surprisingly spunky for a dog who's estimated to be between nine and ten years old. This Italian Greyhound mix loves making his foster mom laugh because he can be goofy and hilarious when the mood strikes. He gets along great with all dogs and has clearly decided that life is pretty good now that someone actually cares about him. Morrie would love to find his very own person to show him what it's like to be a beloved family member instead of an afterthought. He's got energy and personality to spare, so don't let the senior label fool you. Dogs who come back from situations like his often have the biggest hearts once they realize they're safe. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application at www.rmgdogs.org if you want to meet this resilient little character.
scoring_notes:
• Incredible transformation story from neglect to thriving creates strong reader connection
• Senior status means longer shelter stays and urgent need for placement
• Resilient personality despite past trauma showcases dogs' capacity for forgiveness

blurb:
Junior's family suddenly decided they were allergic to him after years of living together. That was their excuse anyway. He ended up scared and alone at the county shelter, watching people walk past his kennel over and over. Black dog syndrome is real, and Junior knows it firsthand.

But here's what makes Junior special: he loves ice cream runs. Bruster's and Pelicans are his favorite spots, and after a good outing, he goes home with his foster friend Katie and becomes the perfect houseguest. He's house trained, well mannered, and genuinely loving once he's in a home setting. He just needed someone to give him a real chance.

Junior does best as the only dog and with older kids, but if you're looking for a loyal companion who's already proven he can be the perfect house dog, this is your guy. He's been through enough disappointment. He's ready for someone who won't change their mind about him.

Our Pals Place is a rescue in Marietta. You can find Junior on Petfinder and submit an application to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Black dog syndrome and repeated rejection creates strong advocacy angle for newsletter
• Proven house manners and ice cream runs provide concrete lifestyle appeal
• Long wait time at shelter generates reader urgency to help end his disappointment

blurb:
Luke's story started with tragedy but he's making the best of it. His mom Miss Daisy's owner died unexpectedly, and when no family stepped up to help, the boyfriend dropped Miss Daisy and her two puppies at the county shelter. They sat there for weeks waiting for someone to care. Finally, a rescue stepped in and gave them a real chance.

Luke is the fun-loving, playful one of the bunch. He's got a Malinois face but he's smaller than your typical shepherd, which actually makes him pretty perfect for a lot of homes. He got lucky timing-wise too. He arrived right when the rescue was running their summer camp, so he's been getting walks on trails and tons of attention from kids all summer.

He's a young dog who's been through some upheaval but came out loving and ready to bond with his own family. If you want a shepherd personality in a more manageable size, Luke might be exactly what you're looking for.

Our Pals Place is a rescue in Marietta. You can find Luke on Petfinder and submit an application to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Tragic family loss story creates immediate emotional connection with readers
• Ideal size and temperament combination appeals to broad range of potential adopters
• Summer camp socialization shows investment in his development and readiness for adoption

blurb:
His name is Pork Tenderloin and yes, that's actually what they're calling him. He's a handsome guy who came from a county shelter and he's what you'd call a little misunderstood. He likes attention, but only when he's in the mood for it. When he wants affection, he'll come find you. When he doesn't, he'd rather you leave him alone.

He can get overwhelmed faster than some cats, so he needs a calm house and someone who gets that about him. He's not mean or aggressive, just particular about his space and his boundaries. If you've had cats before and you know how to read their body language, you'll probably do fine with him.

With the right person who understands what he needs, he has the potential to be a really special cat. He just needs someone patient enough to let him warm up on his own terms. Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Memorable name creates immediate reader interest and shareability
• Requires specific home environment which significantly limits potential adopters
• Misunderstood pet angle appeals to readers who love underdogs

blurb:
Goober is a chocolate lab with a name that fits his personality perfectly. He's a little cautious around loud noises and new people at first, but hand him a few treats and watch him turn into a completely different dog. Once he decides you're good people, you're going to get the full Goober experience.

He loves his rope toys, gives kisses freely, and apparently sings. The rescue says he does a great Chewbacca impression, which honestly sounds like something you need to see for yourself. He's goofy in the best possible way and has the kind of personality that's going to make you laugh every day.

If you've got patience for the initial shyness and want a dog who's going to bring some genuine entertainment to your life, Goober is going to be perfect. He just needs someone who gets that the best dogs are usually a little weird.

Our Pals Place is a rescue in Marietta. You can find Goober on Petfinder and submit an application to meet him.
scoring_notes:
• Entertaining personality quirks like singing create memorable and shareable content
• Chocolate lab with goofy traits appeals to wide demographic of potential adopters
• Straightforward adoption case without major barriers or special requirements

blurb:
Brie is a 5-year-old orange and white beauty who's got her priorities straight. She loves scheduled meals, gentle brushing sessions, and parking herself next to your laptop while you work. She's currently about 14.5 pounds and would benefit from a slow, steady slim-down, but she's not a grazer so portioned meals work perfectly for her.

She's a little shy when you first meet her, but pull out a brush for some gentle head and neck scratching and she'll melt into the happiest purrs. She's the kind of cat who'll politely request pets while you're trying to get things done. She's comfortable with dogs as long as they give her space, but she's not a fan of other cats and would do best as an only cat.

Because high energy can overwhelm her, Brie thrives in quieter homes with adults or calm older teens. If you're patient and ready to cheer on a sweetheart as she blossoms, she's going to be an amazing companion.

To adopt Brie, fill out an adoption application on Three Paws Rescue's website. They're based in Chamblee and handle adoptions through their online process.
scoring_notes:
• Perfect work-from-home companion who loves laptop supervision duty
• Specific care needs may limit adopter pool but creates strong match potential
• Shy cats often wait longer, needs patient adopter to help her blossom

blurb: Panda and her eight puppies were held as part of a court case before the rescue could take them in. Based on the scars she carries, her previous situation wasn't good, but that's all behind her now. She's about seven years old but don't tell her that. This girl still loves to get out and romp and play with dogs half her age, as long as they're big enough to keep up with her. She's not a fan of small breed dogs, but she's great with all people and would love nothing more than a comfy sofa to curl up on for naps. Panda has successfully completed treatment for heartworm disease and is officially free and clear, ready for her perfect forever home. She's the kind of dog who's been through the worst and come out the other side still believing that people can be good. If you've got a big dog energy household and a couch with her name on it, Panda is ready to show you just how grateful she can be. Email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application at www.rmgdogs.org to meet this resilient girl.
scoring_notes:
• Powerful survival story with court case and medical recovery creates urgency
• Multiple challenges (age, size requirements, past trauma) suggest extended shelter time
• Inspiring resilience and gratitude message resonates strongly with readers

blurb:
Patriot is Good Mews's unofficial mascot, and once you meet him you'll understand why. He's super outgoing and friendly, loves people, and actually enjoys living at the shelter because he gets constant attention from staff and visitors. He was adopted as a kitten but got hit by a car after his owners let him outside. The accident left him with some medical issues including incontinence, and his owners couldn't handle his care.

He had surgery to repair his hip and had his tail amputated, but he gets around just fine. The volunteers say he leaves little accidents around the shelter that he can't control, but he's such a wonderful cat that everyone understands. He loves playing with the more active cats and will probably jump in your lap for snuggles if you sit down near him.

He needs someone who can handle his medical needs and won't mind cleaning up after him sometimes. In return, you get an incredibly social, loving cat who's already proven he can bounce back from anything. Good Mews has him in their special programs for cats with ongoing needs, so they'll work with you on his care.
scoring_notes:
• Incredible resilience story with shelter mascot status creates powerful emotional appeal
• Long-term resident with special needs generates urgency and reader sympathy
• Medical requirements present significant barrier to adoption despite compelling story

blurb:
Maple Leaf is a kitten with basically no bio, so we're going to have to go with what we can see. She's got that classic kitten look that says she's probably into chasing things, pouncing on your feet under blankets, and falling asleep in the middle of whatever chaos she just created.

She's at DeKalb County Animal Services, which means she's probably been there a little while and is ready to go home with someone. Kittens this age are usually pretty adaptable and will settle into whatever routine you set up for them. They're also at that perfect age where they're playful enough to be entertaining but old enough to not need round-the-clock care.

Since her listing doesn't have much detail, you'll want to visit in person to get a feel for her personality. Every kitten is different, and spending a few minutes with her will tell you everything you need to know about whether she's your kind of troublemaker.

DeKalb County Animal Services is in Chamblee. Call ahead to make sure she's still available and to ask about their adoption process.
scoring_notes:
• High-appeal kitten with universal charm and adaptability
• County shelter pet needs visibility to find home quickly
• Minimal backstory limits emotional connection with readers
